_note: Reference-driven (IP-Adapter). Pair with an uploaded source screenshot / official key art (payload.reference_artifact) and the base checkpoint borrows THAT game's look — the real answer to 'never got the game I wanted', with no per-game LoRA and no training. The REFERENCE carries the game's identity (creatures, palette, style); the PROMPT carries the subject, emotion and shot. Needs the ComfyUI IP-Adapter nodes + h94/IP-Adapter SDXL models on the worker. Swap the checkpoint (docs/MODELS.md) to match the game's style family.
template: {subject}, {emotion} expression, {composition}, YouTube thumbnail key art that matches the reference game's art style, palette and creatures, {lighting}, ultra-detailed, crisp high contrast, punchy saturated colors, bold readable space left for a title
negative: text, letters, words, watermark, blurry, soft, low contrast, washed out, extra limbs, cropped body, generic stock fantasy, unrelated game

composition: subject filling the left third, face large
extreme close-up with space on the right

rubric.reference_match: 1-5 — does it read as the SAME game as the reference?
